events.notes: ICO participant allocation distributed at mainnet genesis; no lock-up applied.
IOHK allocation (~5.18B total across IOHK, CF, Emurgo) distributed at genesis with no on-chain time-lock; figure here is Emurgo portion.
Cardano Foundation allocation distributed at genesis; no on-chain vesting contract enforced.
IOHK allocation distributed at genesis; no on-chain time-lock; subject to informal multi-year commitment per IOHK agreements.
Shelley era began; staking rewards started drawing from the ~13.88B ADA reserve at ~0.3% of remaining reserve per 5-day epoch, continuing indefinitely.
Ongoing reserve emissions as of 2024; approximately 8–9B ADA remaining in reserve, emitting ~300M–400M ADA annually at current decay rate.

summary: Cardano had no traditional time-locked vesting schedule: the vast majority of ADA (ICO participants ~57.6%, IOHK ~11.5%, Emurgo ~4.9%, Cardano Foundation ~1.4%) was distributed at genesis on 2017-09-29 with no on-chain lock-up contracts. The only ongoing 'unlock pressure' comes from staking reward emissions drawn from a reserve pool of ~13.88B ADA (originally ~30.9% of max supply), which decays at roughly 0.3% of the remaining reserve per 5-day epoch — meaning annual emissions are declining over time and will asymptotically approach zero. There are no upcoming cliff unlocks or investor tranches to monitor.
caveats: 1) IOHK, Emurgo, and Cardano Foundation founding allocations had informal/contractual (not on-chain) commitments about usage; the exact terms of those agreements are not fully public, so any internal vesting schedules between those entities are NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER. 2) tokenunlocks.app does not carry a Cardano vesting page, confirming no structured on-chain unlock schedule exists. 3) Precise current reserve balance requires querying a Cardano node or explorer (e.g. adapools.org) in real time; the figures cited here are approximations from public documentation. 4) Messari's detailed token unlock data for Cardano is behind their Pro paywall.
